title: Methods of Sterilization MCQ – ORT Textbook
source: Textbook for Operation Room Technicians – Methods of Sterilization (Pages 363–368)

questions.question: In a pre-vacuum sterilizer, how is air removed from the chamber?
questions.explanation: In a pre-vacuum sterilizer, air is first removed by a vacuum pump. This method is better.

questions.question: What material is the autoclaving machine made of?
questions.explanation: The autoclaving machine is made of strong steel. The shape is either horizontal or vertical.

questions.question: Horizontal designs of autoclaves are commonly used in which area?
questions.explanation: Horizontal designs are commonly used in CSSD (Central Sterile Supply Department).

questions.question: What is the function of the jacket on the outer surface of the autoclave chamber?

options.C: To maintain the desired temperature by releasing steam

questions.explanation: On the outer surface of the chamber is a jacket in which steam is released. This jacket maintains the desired temperature.

questions.question: Where should the solid load made of metal be kept during autoclave loading?
questions.explanation: The solid load made of metal should be kept on the bottom rack.

questions.question: Where should the instrument tray be kept during autoclave loading?
questions.explanation: The instrument tray should be kept in the top rack.

questions.question: Why are solid articles like drum, bottles, and basin kept horizontal in the autoclave?
questions.explanation: Solid articles like drum, bottles, basin etc. are kept horizontal so that air is removed.

questions.question: What is the first step involved in autoclave sterilization?

options.D: Air is removed from the chamber

questions.explanation: First air is removed from the chamber after loading is done, then temperature is set.

questions.question: How many times is the vacuum process repeated in a pre-vacuum chamber to remove all air?
questions.explanation: This process is repeated thrice to remove all the air out of the packs.

questions.question: What is the sterilization time at 121°C in an autoclave?
questions.explanation: At 121°C, the sterilization time is 15 minutes.

questions.question: What is the sterilization time at 126°C in an autoclave?
questions.explanation: At 126°C, the sterilization time is 10 minutes.

questions.question: What is the sterilization time at 134°C in an autoclave?
questions.explanation: At 134°C, the sterilization time is 03 minutes.

questions.question: The time from start of sterilization till the desired temperature is achieved is called:
questions.explanation: Lag period is the time taken from starting of sterilization to the time till it is maintained — i.e., achievement of desired temperature.

questions.question: What is holding time in autoclave sterilization?

options.C: The time from starting of set temperature to the time of sterilization

questions.explanation: Holding time is the time from starting of set temperature to the time of sterilization. This time is considered as sterilization time.

questions.question: What happens if steam condenses on the packs in the chamber after sterilization?

options.B: Water drops form on the packs

questions.explanation: To remove the steam and drying of object after sterilization — otherwise the steam will condense on the packs in the form of water drops on the chamber.

questions.question: After sterilization, what type of air is pushed into the autoclave chamber?
questions.explanation: Now cool air is pushed in the chamber. This should be cleaned and filtered.

questions.question: The hard water should NOT be used for sterilization because:
questions.explanation: The hard water should not be used for sterilization otherwise flakes are deposited. Now the pressure of air is reduced and brought to normal.

questions.question: When should the sterilizer NOT be opened after a cycle?

options.B: Until the steam has come out and it has become cold

questions.explanation: Do not open the sterilizer till steam has come out and it has become cold.

questions.question: Where should cooling objects NOT be placed after unloading from the sterilizer?
questions.explanation: The cooling items should not be kept on the cold metal surface otherwise the water would condense inside the pack.

questions.question: Packs unloaded from the sterilizer should NOT be kept on:
questions.explanation: Packs should not be kept on the floor.

questions.question: How far should sterile items be stored from the floor?
questions.explanation: Sterile items should be stored at least 8-10 inches away from the floor.

questions.question: How far should sterile items be stored from the ceiling?
questions.explanation: Sterile items should be stored at least 18 inches away from the ceiling.

questions.question: How far should sterile items be stored from the outside walls?
questions.explanation: Sterile items should be stored at least 2 inches from the outside walls.

questions.question: Each autoclave or sterilizer should have which of the following monitors?

options.C: Thermometer for thermocouple type, pressure of boiler jacket and chamber, and recording facility

questions.explanation: Each autoclave or sterilizer should have thermometer for thermocouple type, pressure of boiler jacket and chamber, thermometer for discharge lines and packs, and recording facility for above parameters on a graph paper.

questions.question: What essential information should be recorded for each sterilization cycle?

options.C: Number, date, time, and name of the person doing sterilization
options.D: Only the name of the person

questions.explanation: There should be number, date, time, and name of the person doing sterilization.

questions.question: What is it essential to do before sterilization, otherwise the process will not be quite effective?
questions.explanation: It is essential to remove air from the chamber before sterilization otherwise it will not be quite effective.

questions.question: How many freshly washed thin towels are used in the Bowie-Dick test?
questions.explanation: For this test take 8-10 freshly washed thin towels with honey comb design, or some plain cotton sheets.

questions.question: What should be the size of towels used in the Bowie-Dick test?
questions.explanation: The size of towel should be 1 m × 0.6 m. This should be folded in a square fashion.

questions.question: What should be the height of the pile of towels in the Bowie-Dick test?
questions.explanation: A pile of such towels should be made which should be 0.28 meters in height.

questions.question: Where should the autoclaved tape be placed in the Bowie-Dick test pack?

options.C: In the centre of the pile
options.D: At the bottom of the pile

questions.explanation: Autoclaved tape has been stuck horizontally, should be kept in the centre of the pile.

questions.question: The Bowie-Dick test pile should be wrapped in:

options.C: Double layer of cloth or paper

questions.explanation: Now this pile should be wrapped in double layer of cloth or paper.

questions.question: The Bowie-Dick test sterilization cycle should be done in:
questions.explanation: One sterilization cycle should be done in empty autoclave.

questions.question: In the Bowie-Dick test, if the pack is smoky after opening, it means:

options.C: The air has not been removed completely

questions.explanation: Now open the pack and see the colour, if it is smoky, it means the air has not been removed completely.

questions.question: In the vacuum leak test, after 10 minutes the pressure should not be more than:
questions.explanation: Vacuum leak test – When vacuum process is completed, an increase in pressure by 20 mm after 5 minutes may occur but after 10 minutes it should not be more than 10 mm.

questions.question: In the chemical test for monitoring sterilization, what happens to the tape?

options.C: The colour of tape turns black

questions.explanation: Chemical test – It is done by a tape, which should be put in each pack. The colour of tape turns black. The results are instantly known by the change in colour.

questions.question: How often should the biological test for monitoring sterilization be done?
questions.explanation: Biological test for Monitoring – This should be done once a week.

questions.question: In the biological test, which bacterium is used to check steam sterilization effectiveness?
questions.explanation: Here, bacteria stearothermophilus is kept in the sterilizer. It is very difficult to kill these bacteria and if it is destroyed in the sterilizer, then there is no doubt about the effectiveness of the sterilization.

questions.question: How often should vacuum test be done for steam sterilization?
questions.explanation: Do vacuum test once a week.

questions.question: For how long should the thermograph record be maintained?
questions.explanation: Maintain thermograph record for two years.

questions.question: Which of the following is a harmful effect of autoclave sterilization on sharp instruments?
questions.explanation: The edges of sharp instruments get blunted. Metal instruments lose their surface and overall life of instruments gets shortened.

questions.question: What does TSSU stand for?
questions.explanation: TSSU stands for Theatre Sterile Supply Unit.

questions.question: What does flash sterilization mean?
questions.explanation: Flash Sterilization means quick sterilization. This unit is usually installed in OT so that instant sterilization can be done in case of need.

questions.question: What is the flash sterilization time for non-porous load at 132°C?
questions.explanation: Non-porous load requires 132°C for 3 minutes in flash sterilization.

questions.question: What is the flash sterilization time for porous load (clothes, towel, rubber, catheter) at 132°C?
questions.explanation: Porous load e.g. Clothes, towel, rubber, catheter requires 132°C for 14 minutes.

questions.question: Which items are NOT sterilized by flash sterilization in TSSU?
questions.explanation: Here only surgical instruments are autoclaved. Implants are not sterilized by this method.

questions.question: Flash sterilization is typically done when:

options.B: An instrument has fallen on the ground during surgery

questions.explanation: This sterilization is done only in case if an instrument has fallen on the ground and it is needed for the surgery again or any other instrument needed during surgery, which is not required normally.

questions.question: Instruments sterilized by flash sterilization should be:
questions.explanation: The instruments sterilized by this should be used instantly otherwise these become unsterile again.

questions.question: What should NOT be kept in the room where the sterilizer is installed?
questions.explanation: The dirty linen should not be kept in this room where sterilizer is installed.

questions.question: On what surface should instruments be kept in flash sterilization?
questions.explanation: The instruments should be kept on the wire mesh or perforated tray.

questions.question: Dry heat sterilization does NOT contain:
questions.explanation: Sterilization with dry heat – This heat does not contain water.

questions.question: How are items packed for dry heat sterilization?

options.C: In metal containers or wrapped in aluminium foil

questions.explanation: Packing for dry heat – Such items are kept in metal containers or wrapped in aluminium foil.

questions.question: Dry heat sterilization is sometimes still used in which settings?
questions.explanation: This method is not popular these days. This is sometimes used in dental settings.

questions.question: What is the major disadvantage of dry heat sterilization?

options.B: It is a slow and uneven process

questions.explanation: Major disadvantage of dry heat sterilization is that it is slow & uneven process.

questions.question: In a hot air oven, hot air is circulated by:
questions.explanation: Hot Air Oven – This equipment gives dry heat. Here hot air is circulated in the oven by a fan.

questions.question: What is the sterilization time in a hot air oven at 160°C?
questions.explanation: At 160°C, the sterilization time is 60 minutes.

questions.question: What is the sterilization time in a hot air oven at 170°C?
questions.explanation: At 170°C, the sterilization time is 40 minutes.

questions.question: What is the sterilization time in a hot air oven at 180°C?
questions.explanation: At 180°C, the sterilization time is 20 minutes.

questions.question: Which of the following items can be sterilized in a hot air oven?

options.C: Cream, oil, Vaseline, powder, and sharp-edged instruments

questions.explanation: Items sterilizable with hot air oven are cream, oil, Vaseline, instruments with multiple joints like scissors, syringes, powder, air cushions, sharp edged instruments. Ophthalmology instruments can also be sterilized.

questions.question: Liquid materials in a hot air oven are sterilized in:
questions.explanation: Liquid materials are sterilized in bottles.

questions.question: What is the storage life of items packed in single layer cotton cloth on an open shelf?
questions.explanation: Single layer of cotton cloth on open shelf has a storage life of 3 to 14 days.

questions.question: What is the storage life of items packed in single layer cotton cloth on a closed shelf?
questions.explanation: Single layer of cotton cloth on closed shelf has a storage life of 14 to 21 days.

questions.question: What is the storage life of items packed in double layer cotton cloth on a closed shelf?
questions.explanation: Double layer of cotton cloth on closed shelf has a storage life of 56 to 77 days.

questions.question: Ionizing radiation sterilization can be with:

options.B: Electron beam, gamma radiation, and X-ray or sub-atomic particles

questions.explanation: Ionizing radiation can be with Electron beam, gamma radiation, and X-ray or sub-atomic particles.

questions.question: Why is ionizing radiation sterilization not suitable for use in hospitals?
questions.explanation: Disposable items like syringes are sterilized by this method as this is not suitable for use in hospitals but it is used commercially as the equipment is costly.

questions.question: Which plastic material can be damaged by ionizing radiation?
questions.explanation: This process can damage some plastic materials like PVC. Advice from manufacturer should be taken before resterilizing any disposable item.

questions.question: An advantage of radiation sterilization is that items can be stored for:

options.D: An indefinite period till the seal is broken

questions.explanation: Items sterilized by this can be stored for indefinite period till the seal is broken or packet is torn.

questions.question: Radiation sterilization gives good penetration and:
questions.explanation: This gives good penetration and no residual radioactivity is present.

questions.question: Which material is NOT suitable for ionizing radiation sterilization?
questions.explanation: Equipment made of or containing metal, polythene, paper, wood, cotton, PVC, nylon, terylene etc. remain unaffected. Rubber is also not suitable.

questions.question: Filtration is the method of choice for sterilizing:

options.B: Antibiotic solutions, toxic chemicals, radio isotopes, and vaccines

questions.explanation: Filtration is the method of choice for sterilizing antibiotic solutions, toxic chemicals, radio isotopes, vaccines etc.

questions.question: Filters used in sterilization by filtration work by:

options.C: Physical trapping of particles larger than pore size

questions.explanation: Filters work by physical trapping of particles larger than pore size.
